title: Proof
description: The core methods of mathematical proof used throughout A-level: proof by deduction, proof by exhaustion, and disproof by counter-example.
objectives: Explain what it means to prove a mathematical statement, and distinguish a proof from a check of examples
Construct a proof by deduction: a chain of algebraic or logical steps from agreed definitions to a conclusion
Use algebraic representations of odd, even, and consecutive integers to prove statements about them
Construct a proof by exhaustion by checking every case in a finite set
Disprove a false general statement by finding a single counter-example
Judge which proof technique (deduction, exhaustion, or counter-example) is appropriate for a given claim
